name: New Zealand
intro: New Zealand's project market is small enough that certification travels fast: government digital programmes in Wellington, the infrastructure catch-up across Auckland, and a surprisingly deep tech-export sector all draw from the same limited pool of certified professionals, which keeps demand — and recognition — high. PMP and Scrum credentials are both understood instantly by NZ employers, and many professionals certify with trans-Tasman careers in mind. There are live online batches all year for New Zealand — pick a Saturday–Sunday weekend or a Wednesday–Thursday weekday cohort — each timed to fall within normal New Zealand working-day hours; the schedule below gives the precise local start and end for every date. The format stays: live instruction, exam application support, mocks, and trainer access until you certify.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in New Zealand?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in New Zealand?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across New Zealand.
